description_long: This object is a classic box-shaped television with a matte black exterior and rounded edges, reminiscent of mid-to-late 20th-century CRT models. The front features a large, slightly recessed screen displaying static, surrounded by a thick, black bezel. Two thin metal antennas extend upward from a dome-shaped mount on the top, giving it a retro appearance. The back panel has a set of horizontal ventilation slits, likely for heat dissipation, and the sides are smooth and unadorned. The television appears to be constructed primarily from plastic with metallic elements for the antennas and internal components. Its overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or modern features such as flat panels or touch controls.
description: A retro, boxy black television with a static-filled screen, metal antennas, and a thick bezel. The design is simple, with rounded edges and a vented back panel.
description_view_0: The left side of the television is visible, showing a smooth, featureless black surface and the top-mounted antennas.
description_view_1: The front view displays the static-filled screen, thick black bezel, and the antennas extending from the top dome.
description_view_2: The right side mirrors the left, with a plain black surface and the antennas visible from above.
description_view_3: The back view reveals horizontal ventilation slits and the dome where the antennas are mounted.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, matte black plastic bezel with rounded corners and no visible styling features
